title: EU AI Act — Full Entry into Force
summary: The world's first comprehensive AI regulation entered into force, establishing a risk-tiered framework that subjects high-risk AI systems to mandatory conformity assessments, imposes transparency obligations on general-purpose AI models, and prohibits specific AI applications outright. The Act applies to any provider or deployer operating within the EU market, regardless of where they are incorporated.
legal_mechanism: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 under Article 114 TFEU (internal market harmonisation). Binding directly on all 27 EU member states without transposition. Phased application: prohibited practices from February 2025; GPAI model obligations from August 2025; high-risk system obligations from August 2026. Enforcement via national market surveillance authorities with fines up to €35M or 7% of global annual turnover.
economic_consequence: Compliance costs estimated at €400,000–€1.2M per high-risk AI system for initial conformity assessment, plus ongoing audit costs. The EU AI Office projects a net GDP impact of +€271B over five years through productivity gains, offset by near-term compliance drag on EU-headquartered AI firms. Global companies face a binary choice: segment EU AI deployments or standardise globally on EU requirements (the Brussels Effect). A new AI compliance industry — auditors, notified bodies, legal advisory — is already forming, with projected market size of €2–4B by 2027.
